prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo HEAT-repeat protein (150–200 residues) that selectively binds the KRAS G12D mutant over wild-type KRAS by exploiting the altered switch II pocket geometry introduced by the G12D substitution. The designed protein should achieve at least 50-fold selectivity for KRAS G12D versus wild-type. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion hallucination targeting the KRAS G12D switch II pocket, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with selectivity filter for the aspartate side chain, (3) FoldX stability scoring and Rosetta delta-affinity prediction. Include experimental validation by SPR and cellular target engagement assays.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: KRAS G12D; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: RAS GTPase oncogenic driver (constitutively active mutant)
